what_it_does: Static HTML/CSS mockup of the desktop C1 Inbox operating surface: a three-lane right-hand triage panel (live escalations, batched themes, resolved-for-you audit), plus a header with presence pill, a left process/content rail, a waiting canvas, and a footer. It visualizes the intended operator triage state with value-altitude framing, drill affordances, and data-ui-ref annotations.
observations: Extensive HTML comment block maps every lane and behavior to runtime code (suite.py, governance.py) and user-journey moments.
Three inbox lanes are rendered: live escalations (4 items), a batched review theme (3 members), and resolved-for-you audit rows (2 items).
Header presence pill reads 'focus · awaiting your approval' and the footer shows '4 awaiting you · 2 resolved (audit)'.
A visible 'test-origin excluded: 14' note is shown, framed as fail-loud-legible filtering.
Each live item has a kind badge, ID, COA value-altitude line, recommendation, 'drill to ground' link, and an action button.
The batched lane is presented as a pure regrouping of live escalations by action, with member IDs explicitly listed.
data-ui-ref attributes annotate UI elements throughout, suggesting a UI inventory or resolver.
No JavaScript is present; tabs, buttons, and drill actions are non-functional in this static prototype.
Uses CSS variables from ../design-system.css and a grid layout with areas top/rail/canvas/foot/panel.
Example items include a build_result_review, a surfaced node result, a generative idea, and a panel proposal.
standouts: The file functions as both a visual mockup and a code-anchored behavioral specification.
It foregrounds test-origin exclusion as a counted, visible governance signal rather than silent filtering.
Value-altitude framing (COA line before raw detail) and a 'drill to ground' affordance are central to every live item.
The batched lane is explicitly a regrouping, not a separate pile, with invariant comments.
conventions: Follows the project's design-system.css variable naming and color semantics (await, acc, cache, etc.).
Uses a CSS grid layout with named areas (top, rail, canvas, foot, panel).
Annotates UI elements with data-ui-ref attributes for traceability.
Includes standard HTML boilerplate: doctype, lang, charset, viewport.
Breaks the convention that mockups stay visual-only by embedding a large runtime-behavior commentary.
Uses an inline style block for prototype-specific styling, which is typical for mockups.
concerns: Large comment block references external code line numbers that will drift and become misleading.
Static prototype has interactive-looking buttons and tabs with no behavior, risking confusion in demos.
Hardcoded counts and item IDs may be mistaken for live data.
Canvas 'dim-note' repeats the awaiting count, creating a potential divergence point.
Interactive-looking elements lack ARIA roles, labels, or focus states.
notes: A desktop inbox mockup that doubles as a grounded specification for a three-lane operator triage surface, complete with value framing, batching, and visible exclusion accounting.
questions: Are the data-ui-ref values consumed by a runtime UI resolver, or are they only mockup annotations?
Is the right panel's normal width (360px) the canonical inbox width across all views?
Which file or component renders the drilled-into canvas state when an item is selected?
Does design-system.css guarantee all CSS variables used here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a static visual mockup of the C1 desktop inbox with the three documented lanes, counts, presence state, and test-origin exclusion note.
apparent_themes: Operator-attention triage: show only what needs the operator, pre-filtered.
Batch handling by shared action theme.
intention_signals: To provide a concrete, code-anchored visual reference for the chief-of-staff inbox that teaches the intended triage semantics (value-first, drillable, batched, audited) and aligns design, frontend, and runtime implementations.
novelty: The mockup embeds a detailed runtime specification in comments, treating the HTML file itself as a living bridge between design and backend behavior; it also foregrounds test-origin exclusion as a visible count rather than hiding it.
contribution: A clear three-lane inbox layout with value-altitude item framing, visible exclusion accounting, batched-theme grouping, and data-ui-ref annotations that can seed a component inventory or UI resolver.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS desktop mockup (C1) of a three-lane operator inbox showing live escalations, batched action themes, resolved audit rows, a test-origin exclusion count, and presence status.
intention_for_embedding: To visualize and specify the standing triage surface where an operator sees pre-filtered, value-framed escalations and can drill to ground or handle themed batches.
